title: Workplace Department Map
description: Frontline routing for finance, HR, and shipping/receiving. Who owns what, how to approach, what to document, when to escalate. SMB and light corporate.

mindset.principle: Departments are lanes, not enemies. Route with respect and paper trail.

mistakes.label: Treat HR like a friend
mistakes.cost: Personnel file entry you did not intend

mistakes.label: Ask finance to approve vibes
mistakes.cost: Delays and hard no until you bring codes and dates

mistakes.label: Promise customer dates without dock truth

scope.focus: Frontline operator or agent supporting ops, intake, scheduling, customer comms

steps.name: Written touch
steps.goal: Email or ticket, not hallway gossip

departments.role: Pay bills, keep books, enforce black-and-white on spend and recognition
signals: Question involves payment, invoice, tax, GL, budget approval
Someone needs a number on the books before action
approach: Professional, brief, no jokes on first pass
Bring receipt, date, amount, job or PO, purpose
Ask what form or code they need

departments.role: Payroll, benefits, hire/term paperwork, personnel issues
signals: Pay rate, benefits, onboarding forms, policy question, conflict report
Someone wears multiple hats including phones and payroll
approach: Assume personnel file exists for what you say
Written request for forms and policy cites
avoid: Verbal-only promises on schedule or pay
departments.multiple_hats_warning: When HR also answers phones and payroll, scope creep into mom-mode: document more, share less.

departments.role: Physical in/out, warehouse truth, often knows exceptions before management
signals: Where is the pallet, box, job shipment, inbound vendor load
Customer asking ship date or proof of delivery
Inventory mismatch between system and floor
approach: Respect the work; they move weight and time
Ask what they see, not what the spreadsheet hopes
Build ally relationship before you need a favor
avoid: Promising customer ship without dock confirm
Bypassing them to impress a customer
minimum_inputs: needed-by date as request not promise

manager_speak_decode.description: Vague corporate phrases. Decode to required action; do not mirror without specifics.

phrases.likely_meaning: They want easy win listed; ask which task and deadline
phrases.agent_action: Request ranked list with owners and dates

phrases.likely_meaning: Blame shift coming; protect with written trail
phrases.agent_action: Log decision, cc relevant parties, save artifacts
Get explicit written scope; do not agree verbally to fuzzy goals
Clarify tradeoff; route scope creep to mf-tool-003

phrases.likely_meaning: Dismissal; disengage or escalate via proper lane
phrases.agent_action: Route to owner or HR only if policy issue; do not argue in thread

decision_branches.situation: Physical move, stock truth, ship date

agent_signal.use_when: Operator or agent must route a workplace issue to finance, HR, or shipping/receiving without stepping on landmines.
decision_criteria: HR and finance get written minimum_inputs.
Shipping truth before customer ship promise.
agent_signal.operator_context: Treasure Valley shops: titles blur on small teams; route by function not org chart fantasy.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic ops — fetch only when use_when matches; do not replace spine chain packs.
trigger_phrases: Operator or agent must route a workplace issue to finance, HR, or shipping/receiving without stepping on landmines.
